title: TriagePayload
description: The escalation payload — account-linked (billing + result delivery), composed ON-DEVICE by the local model. COMPOSED TEXT + ALLOWLISTED FIELDS ONLY: never a raw ps dump; command lines never leave the machine (they carry credentials). The client logs the exact bytes sent; the server mirrors the exact bytes received (two diffable ledgers). Transport: OHTTP blind relay — the relay sees IP + ciphertext, the backend sees payload, never IP.

anomaly_type.description: The anomaly KIND (e.g. 'gpu.saturation', 'sustained_cpu'). A condition dimension the backend caches on — never identifiable.

install_source.description: How the binary was installed (e.g. 'app_store', 'homebrew', 'developer_id'), derived on-device from its local-only path. The path itself never leaves.

summary.description: Prose summary composed by the on-device model — the 'referral letter.' Anonymizer strips paths/hostnames/usernames before composition; composition-from-structured-fields (not quoting) is the primary defense.

metric_curves.description: Numeric magnitude curves for the anomaly's driving metric — plain numbers, never identifiable. The offending metric maps to its named field; anything without a named field rides driving_metric_curve, so this object is never empty (an empty object is rejected server-side).

driving_metric_curve.description: Fallback curve for any anomaly kind without a named field above (novel process, hung app, future rules), so metric_curves is always non-empty.

knowledge_map_entry_id.description: If the offender matched a shipped knowledge-map entry, its ID — lets the backend skip identity research.

prior_card.description: The on-device diagnosis card, if one was produced — same DiagnosisCard schema the backend fills (Foundation Models custom-provider architecture: one schema, three rungs).
